one_time_description: Lease income of $4,185,920 in 2023 compared to $88,688 in 2022 - appears to be a large non-recurring lease transaction
economic_notes: CFN Holdings LP and Cheam Enterprises Inc. are government business enterprises accounted for by modified equity method. CFN Holdings LP had revenue of $3,780,862 and net income of $2,722,117 for year ended December 31, 2022. Cheam Enterprises Inc. had revenue of $10,955 and net income of $7,918. First Nation provided guarantee of $11,980,000 on a credit facility demand loan to Copper Raven Limited Partnership, a subsidiary of CFN Holdings LP.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED for two reasons: (1) unable to obtain reliable estimates of Asset Retirement Obligations per PS 3280, and (2) no budgeted information included in financial statements. Lease income jumped from $88,688 to $4,185,920 - likely a significant non-recurring transaction. First Nation provided $11,980,000 guarantee on credit facility for Copper Raven Limited Partnership. Three consolidated subsidiaries: Pelólxw Historical Society, CFN Housing Society, Cheam First Nation Trust. Two GBEs on modified equity basis: CFN Holdings LP and Cheam Enterprises Inc. Fiscal year is 2023 not 2024. CMHC transfers of $1,096,003 and Province of BC $114,616 and Other federal $94,353 and First Nation Health Authority $705,909 included in total government transfers calculation. Total government transfers = ISC $3,428,186 + CMHC $1,096,003 + FNHA $705,909 + Province of BC $114,616 + Other federal $94,353 = $5,439,067. OSR = $15,287,379 - $5,439,067 = $9,848,312 (includes lease income, property tax, gaming revenue sharing, earnings from GBEs, interest, rental, investment income, contracts).
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
